propositions: Operation certificates are made asymmetric, signed by an environment's Ed25519 key and verifiable against its public key, via a new substrate module registering rebar.opcert.v1 pinned to the sshsig scheme.

The certificate subject is an in-toto v1 Statement binding ticket id, material fingerprint, and merged-log position (a main commit SHA), so certs cannot be replayed onto a different ticket or mutated fingerprint.

The material fingerprint is the existing canonical gate fingerprint (16-hex sha256 over ticket_id, description, file_impact, children) supplied by the caller and byte-compared as an opaque string by the substrate.

Environment-key rotation uses an explicit-SHA era model where a key is valid at commit P iff added_at_commit is an ancestor of P and revoked_at_commit is not, reusing the shared ancestry rule rather than resolve_event_commit.
The op-cert verifier performs a two-phase check: verify against any historical env key, and if that passes but the era check fails, emit the shared key_not_valid_at_era verdict, else mismatch.
All verdicts (certified, key_not_valid_at_era, mismatch) come from the canonical verify_signature_result enum with no new verdict string introduced.
